channel.title: CyberNetSec - Phishing
channel.description: Latest phishing threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: Exposed Server Reveals Attacker's AI-Powered Phishing Toolkit
item.description:
Researchers at Rapid7 discovered an unsecured server that exposed a sophisticated, AI-assisted toolkit for phishing and malware delivery. The operator, believed to be Russian-speaking, was targeting Windows users in Mexico with an infostealer. The toolkit shows extensive use of Large Language Models...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Identity Attacks Now Top Ransomware Vector, Eclipsing Exploits
item.description:
According to the Sophos 'State of Ransomware 2026' report, compromised identities are now the primary root cause of ransomware attacks, accounting for 79% of incidents. For the first time in four years, malicious email and phishing have surpassed software vulnerability exploitation as the top initia...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: New Email Attacks Use Real Microsoft Login Pages to Bypass MFA
item.description:
Security researchers are warning about increasingly sophisticated email attacks, including the Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform. This adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) attack uses a reverse proxy to present the victim with a real Microsoft login page. When the user authenticates and co...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: New 'CrashStealer' macOS Malware Bypasses Security to Steal Passwords
item.description:
A new information-stealing malware for macOS, dubbed 'CrashStealer,' is actively being used in targeted attacks. The malware evades Apple's Gatekeeper by using a notarized installer and impersonates the legitimate 'CrashReporter.app' to trick users into entering their system password. Once obtained,...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Phishing, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: AssuranceAmerica Data Breach Impacts Over 1.1 Million, Exposing SSNs and Driver's Licenses
item.description:
AssuranceAmerica Managing General Agency, LLC has disclosed a major data breach affecting over 1.1 million individuals. The incident, which occurred in March 2026, stemmed from a targeted attack on an employee. The unauthorized access led to the theft of a vast amount of sensitive policyholder data,...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Phishing, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: SeasonalInvite Phishing Campaign Abuses Legitimate RMM Tools
item.description:
A phishing campaign named 'SeasonalInvite' has been using fake seasonal eCard invitations to trick users into installing legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tools. Active since January 2026, the campaign abuses trusted software like ConnectWise, LogMeIn, and Kaseya to gain persistent r...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Phishing, Cyberattack, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: WatchGuard: Shadow AI, Password Reuse Fueling Business Cyber Risk
item.description:
A July 2026 report from WatchGuard Technologies finds that employee behaviors are creating significant, unmanaged cybersecurity risks for businesses. The study reveals a pervasive "shadow AI" problem, with 64% of employees using unauthorized AI tools for work. This is compounded by persistent poor s...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Phishing, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: Dutch Nationals Suspected in Odido Data Breach Affecting 6 Million Customers
item.description:
Dutch law enforcement suspects local criminals were behind a recent data breach at Odido, a major telecommunications provider in the Netherlands. The attack exposed the personal data of approximately six million customers. The breach was initiated through a phishing attack, where a Dutch-speaking in...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Phishing, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Novel 'Ghostcommit' Attack Hides Malicious Prompts in PNG Files to Fool AI Agents
item.description:
Researchers have unveiled 'Ghostcommit,' a novel proof-of-concept supply chain attack that embeds malicious prompt-injection instructions inside PNG image files. This technique bypasses text-based AI code reviewers, tricking automated AI coding agents into reading sensitive `.env` files and exfiltra...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Threat Intelligence, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: Urgent Patch Advisory: Critical Stored XSS Flaw in Zimbra Allows Account Takeover via Email
item.description:
Zimbra has issued an urgent advisory for a critical stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in its Classic Web Client. The flaw, which has not yet been assigned a CVE, can be exploited by sending a specially crafted email to a target. When the victim opens the malicious email, the embedded s...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Phishing, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →
